data.hijackedTitle: International Journal of Advanced Science and Technology
data.originalTitle:
The authentic journal “International Journal of Advanced Science and Technology” was not identified. But the domain http://sersc.org/journals/index.php/ijast is very likely to be a hijacker
  
  This means that in tracing the source for a potential hijacking journal, the information is clear that the hijacked journal is mimicking another journal, but it is unclear if the mimicked journal (i.e., journal listed in the Original Journal column) is indeed the original source or is itself a hijacked journal from yet another undetermined original source.

data.hijackedTitle: International Journal of Control and Automation
data.originalTitle:
The authentic journal “International Journal of Control and Automation” was not identified. But the domain http://sersc.org/journals/index.php/ijca is very likely to be a hijacker. ISSN portal indicates sersc.org as a website of a journal. But the indexed content in Web of Science doesn't correspond to the published papers on the website 
  
  This means that in tracing the source for a potential hijacking journal, the information is clear that the hijacked journal is mimicking another journal, but it is unclear if the mimicked journal (i.e., journal listed in the Original Journal column) is indeed the original source or is itself a hijacked journal from yet another undetermined original source.

data.hijackedTitle: International Journal of Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity
data.originalTitle:
The authentic journal “International Journal of Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity” was not identified. But the domain sersc.org/journals/index.php/IJDRBC/ is very likely to be a hijacker. ISSN portal indicates sersc.org as a website of a journal with ISSN 2207-8363. According to ISSN Portal this ISSN 2207-8363 is not linked to ISSN 2005-4289. The indexed content in Web of Science doesn't correspond to the published papers on the website http://sersc.org/journals/index.php/IJDRBC/
  
  This means that in tracing the source for a potential hijacking journal, the information is clear that the hijacked journal is mimicking another journal, but it is unclear if the mimicked journal (i.e., journal listed in the Original Journal column) is indeed the original source or is itself a hijacked journal from yet another undetermined original source.
